directory.h3: All Peugeot 407 Variants - Engine Replacement Coverage
directory.intro: A full alphabetical listing of every Peugeot 407 trim covered by our network. All entries link to a dedicated variant page, where prices are further broken down by engine code, model year, and condition type. Click a live link to compare quotes.
directory.label: Variant Directory

variantCoverage.closing: Not sure which variant your Peugeot 407 is? Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact sub-model, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly.

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: The RHR DW10BTED4 replaced earlier 2.0 HDi units, bringing common-rail injection and improved refinement - but DPF/EGR issues define replacement demand.
compatibleVariants: SR, SE, S, Sport, Executive, GT
commonFailures: DPF blockage at 80k-120k miles
EGR valve clogging causing limp mode
Turbo actuator failure on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for RHR Peugeot 407 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Earlier DW10ATED variant without DPF, simpler but less refined; still sought for budget rebuilds on pre-facelift 407 models.
commonFailures: Injector seal leaks causing oil dilution
Glow plug relay failure in cold weather
Timing belt tensioner wear at service intervals
entries.cta: Get quotes for RHK Peugeot 407 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.2L Diesel
entries.history: The 4HT DW12BTED4 brought 170hp to the 407 range; powerful but prone to dual-mass flywheel and injector issues driving replacement enquiries.

entries.title: mass flywheel failure causing clutch judder
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4HT Peugeot 407 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.2L Diesel
entries.history: Updated DW12BTED4 variant with revised ECU mapping; shares core failure patterns with 4HT but benefits from later production refinements.
commonFailures: Turbo oil seal leaks on high-mileage units
DPF regeneration failures in urban use
Swirl flap actuator faults causing power loss
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4HV Peugeot 407 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: The compact DV6C 9HZ brought efficient 109hp diesel power to entry-level 407 trims; turbo and EGR issues dominate replacement searches.
commonFailures: Turbo oil starvation due to clogged feed pipes
EGR cooler leaks causing coolant loss
DPF blockage on short-journey vehicles
entries.cta: Get quotes for 9HZ Peugeot 407 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.7L Diesel
entries.history: The DT17TED4 V6 diesel powered the exclusive 407 Coupe; specialist rebuilds required due to complex twin-turbo layout and high replacement costs.
compatibleVariants: Coupe V6 HDi, Coupe GT V6 HDi
commonFailures: Twin turbo actuator failures causing limp mode

entries.title: pressure fuel pump wear on high mileage
entries.cta: Get quotes for DT17TED4 Peugeot 407 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: The reliable EW10J4 2.0L petrol offered smooth 140hp performance; timing belt maintenance is critical to avoid catastrophic interference damage.
commonFailures: Timing belt failure if not replaced at 60k miles
Coil pack misfires causing rough running
Water pump leaks leading to overheating
entries.cta: Get quotes for EW10J4 Peugeot 407 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.2L Petrol
entries.history: The EW12J4 delivered 158hp for mid-range 407 petrol models; less common than diesel but valued for smoothness and lower DPF concerns.
commonFailures: Ignition coil failures causing misfire codes
Oil consumption on high-mileage units
Thermostat housing cracks leading to coolant loss
entries.cta: Get quotes for EW12J4 Peugeot 407 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Petrol
entries.history: The flagship ES9J4S V6 petrol powered the 407 GT and Coupe; specialist rebuilds address timing belt and coil pack issues common on high-mileage examples.
compatibleVariants: GT, Coupe V6, Coupe GT V6
commonFailures: Timing belt replacement critical at 60k miles
Ignition coil pack failures causing misfires
Oil leaks from valve cover gaskets on age

problems.h4: DPF Blockage - Why Peugeot 407 Diesel Engines Enter Limp Mode
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 407 SR, SE, S, Sport, Executive, GT (2004-2010, RHR, 4HT, 4HV, 9HZ)
problems.rootCause: Short-journey driving prevents DPF regeneration; soot accumulation triggers anti-pollution fault and power restriction.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Forced regeneration or off-car cleaning; EGR valve service
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if DPF not physically damaged; may recur on urban use

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New or refurbished DPF unit fitted with sensors recalibrated
repairOptions.longevity: Restores emissions compliance; labour-intensive on 407 chassis

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded components addressing DPF root causes
repairOptions.longevity: Best value when DPF failure coincides with other wear items; includes 12-month warranty

problems.recommendation: If your 407 has under 100k miles and the DPF is the only issue, cleaning may suffice. However, if you're also experiencing turbo or injector symptoms, a rebuilt engine often proves more cost-effective long-term.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt RHR engine prices for your Peugeot 407

problems.h4: EGR Valve Failure - Loss of Power on Peugeot 407 HDi Engines
problems.rootCause: Carbon buildup from exhaust recirculation clogs the EGR valve and cooler, restricting airflow and triggering limp mode.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Valve removal, ultrasonic cleaning, or blanking plate fitted
repairOptions.longevity: Quick fix for symptoms; may not resolve underlying carbon issues elsewhere

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New EGR valve and cooler assembly fitted with updated gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Restores full power; addresses root cause but doesn't prevent future DPF issues

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Rebuilt engine with revised EGR mapping and upgraded components
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if EGR failure coincides with turbo or injector wear on high-mileage 407

problems.recommendation: EGR cleaning is worthwhile as preventative maintenance. If your 407 has repeated EGR faults alongside turbo symptoms, budget for a rebuilt unit to avoid recurring downtime.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 407 HDi engine replacement with EGR upgrade

problems.h4: Turbo Failure - Oil Contamination Risk on Peugeot 407 2.0/2.2 HDi
problems.rootCause: Turbo oil feed pipe clogging or seal failure leads to oil starvation; failed turbos contaminate the engine with metal debris.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Actuator replacement or recalibration; boost sensor check
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if turbo internals are undamaged; diagnostic essential first

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New or refurbished turbo fitted with revised oil feed pipes and gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Restores boost pressure; requires thorough engine flush to remove metal debris

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo, revised oil system components, and 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best option when turbo failure has contaminated the sump or bearings; prevents repeat failure

problems.recommendation: If diagnostic confirms turbo failure with metal debris in the oil, a rebuilt engine is safer and often cheaper than turbo-only repair on a high-mileage 407.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 2.0 HDi engine prices for your Peugeot 407

problems.h4: Dual Mass Flywheel Failure - Clutch Judder on Peugeot 407 2.2 HDi
problems.rootCause: DMF springs wear from stop-start driving and high torque loads; failure causes vibration, rattling, and potential gearbox damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New dual mass flywheel, clutch plate, release bearing fitted; gearbox removal required
repairOptions.longevity: Restores smooth engagement; labour cost significant on 407 platform

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Rebuilt engine with upgraded DMF-compatible components, supply & fit, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Cost-effective when DMF failure coincides with engine wear; avoids double labour charges

problems.recommendation: If your 407 has under 100k miles and the engine runs well, DMF replacement alone is sensible. On higher-mileage examples, combining engine rebuild with DMF upgrade often saves money versus two separate repairs.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 407 2.2 HDi rebuilt engine with DMF upgrade

problems.h4: Injector Failure & Coding - Rough Running on Peugeot 407 HDi
problems.rootCause: Piezo injector seals degrade or tips carbon up; incorrect coding after replacement causes misfires and excessive smoke.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Injector removal, seal replacement, ECU recoding with specialist tool
repairOptions.longevity: Viable for isolated injector faults; requires accurate coding data

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full set of refurbished injectors fitted and coded; fuel pressure tested
repairOptions.longevity: Restores smooth running; doesn't address underlying fuel contamination causes

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Rebuilt engine with upgraded injectors, revised fuel system components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended when injector failure has caused cylinder scoring or high fuel dilution of oil

problems.recommendation: Single injector faults can be repaired cost-effectively. If multiple injectors have failed or fuel contamination is suspected, a rebuilt engine prevents repeat issues.
problems.cta: Compare Peugeot 407 HDi engine prices with injector upgrade included

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Peugeot 407
finalCta.paragraph: The Peugeot 407's most common engine failures-DPF blockage, EGR faults, turbo issues, DMF wear, and injector problems-often overlap on high-mileage examples. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Peugeot 407 specialists for rebuilt, reconditioned or used engines. Every unit includes upgraded components addressing known failure points, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Peugeot 407 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Peugeot 407 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

items.title: Peugeot 407 1.6L Diesel Engines

items.title: Peugeot 407 2.0L Diesel Engines

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Peugeot 407 engine?
items.answer: Rebuilt Peugeot 407 engines typically cost £900-£2,500 supply-only, depending on engine code and variant. Supply & fit adds £400-£900 labour. Used engines start from £600 but carry higher risk.
keyPoints: 2.0 HDi RHR rebuilt: £900-£2,200 supply-only
2.2 HDi 4HT rebuilt: £1,100-£2,500 supply-only
Supply & fit labour adds £400-£900 typically
Used engines cheaper but variable history
All rebuilt units include minimum 12-month warranty
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Peugeot 407 engine prices now

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a Peugeot 407?
items.answer: Yes, if your 407 is otherwise sound. A £1,200 rebuilt engine can extend life by 50k+ miles, often costing less than buying a comparable used car with unknown history.
keyPoints: Peugeot 407 values: £1,500-£4,000 depending on condition
Rebuilt engine cost: £900-£2,500 typical range
Labour for supply & fit: £400-£900 additional
Warranty protection reduces long-term risk
Avoids depreciation hit of buying another used car
items.cta: Find out if engine replacement makes sense for your Peugeot 407

items.question: What are the most common Peugeot 407 engine problems?
items.answer: DPF blockage, EGR valve failure, turbo issues and dual-mass flywheel wear dominate Peugeot 407 engine failure reports, especially on 2.0 HDi and 2.2 HDi variants.
keyPoints: DPF blockage triggers anti-pollution fault and limp mode
EGR valve clogging causes power loss on diesel models
Turbo oil seal leaks common on high-mileage DW10/DW12
DMF failure on 2.2 HDi causes clutch judder and vibration
Injector coding errors after replacement cause rough running
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 407 engine replacement addressing common failures

items.question: Which Peugeot 407 engine is most reliable?
items.answer: The 2.0 HDi RHR (DW10BTED4) is most reliable when maintained, with fewer complex components than the 2.2 HDi. Petrol EW10J4 units are also robust if timing belts are changed on schedule.
keyPoints: 2.0 HDi RHR: simpler than 2.2, fewer failure points
EW10J4 petrol: reliable if timing belt replaced at 60k miles
Avoid neglected 1.6 HDi with turbo oil feed issues
3.0 V6 petrol reliable but specialist rebuild costs higher
Regular oil changes critical for all diesel variants
items.cta: Compare prices for reliable Peugeot 407 engine replacements

items.question: How long does a Peugeot 407 engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only engines dispatch in 1-3 days. Supply & fit installations typically take 1-2 days at approved UK garages, depending on variant complexity and parts availability.
keyPoints: Used/rebuilt engine dispatch: 1-3 days typical
Supply & fit installation: 1-2 days at approved garage
V6 and Coupe variants may require longer lead times
Post-installation coding and road test included
Nationwide delivery available for supply-only orders
items.cta: Check Peugeot 407 engine supply & fit turnaround times

items.question: Where can I find my Peugeot 407 engine code?
items.answer: Your Peugeot 407 engine code is stamped on the engine block near the gearbox, listed on the V5C logbook, or visible on the VIN plate under the bonnet. RHR, 4HT and 4HV are most common.
keyPoints: Engine code stamped on block near gearbox mount
V5C logbook section V.5 lists engine type/code
VIN plate under bonnet includes engine designation
Common codes: RHR (2.0 HDi), 4HT/4HV (2.2 HDi)
Enter registration above for instant DVLA verification
items.cta: Identify your Peugeot 407 engine code instantly

items.question: What warranty comes with a Peugeot 407 replacement engine?
items.answer: Rebuilt Peugeot 407 engines from EngineMarket suppliers include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Used engines typically offer 3-6 months; confirm terms before purchase.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines: minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
Reconditioned units: 6-12 months typical coverage
Used engines: 3-6 months variable by supplier
Warranty covers parts and labour for manufacturing defects
Always confirm exact terms with chosen supplier pre-purchase
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 407 engine warranty options from UK specialists

items.question: Can I fit a Peugeot 407 engine myself?
items.answer: While technically possible, Peugeot 407 engine replacement requires specialist tools, coding equipment and expertise. Supply & fit via approved garages is recommended for warranty validity and safety.
keyPoints: Engine removal requires transmission support equipment
ECU coding needed post-installation on HDi variants
Incorrect installation voids supplier warranty
Supply & fit service includes coding, fluids and road test
Approved garage network covers all UK regions
items.cta: Find Peugeot 407 engine supply & fit specialists near you

items.question: Are Peugeot 407 engines compatible with other Peugeot models?
items.answer: Some Peugeot 407 engine codes share architecture with 307, 406 and C5 models, but variant-specific mounts, wiring and ECU mapping mean direct swaps require expert verification.
keyPoints: RHR DW10 shared with 307, 406, C5 but mounts differ
ECU coding and immobiliser must match donor vehicle
Wiring loom and sensor compatibility varies by year
Always verify fitment using registration or engine code
EngineMarket specialists confirm compatibility pre-dispatch
items.cta: Verify Peugeot 407 engine compatibility before ordering

items.question: How do I avoid buying a problematic Peugeot 407 engine?
items.answer: Choose rebuilt engines with upgraded components addressing known failures (DPF, EGR, turbo), verify supplier reviews, and ensure a minimum 12-month warranty is included.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines with upgraded DPF/EGR components preferred
Check supplier ratings and Peugeot 407-specific reviews
Request compression test reports for used engines
Ensure warranty covers parts AND labour for defects
Avoid engines without clear failure-point upgrades
